reasoning: Section 4 of the 25th Amendment has never been invoked to strip a President of their powers against their will. Doing so would require the Vice President and a majority of the Cabinet to declare the President unable to discharge his duties, and if the President contests this, both the House and the Senate must approve the action by a two-thirds majority. Given the partisan composition of Congress and the typical loyalty of a Presidentially appointed Cabinet, the chances of this happening to President Trump in 2025 are infinitesimally small. Furthermore, Section 4 technically transfers only the 'powers and duties' of the office, making the VP 'Acting President' rather than permanently removing the President—making a strict 'Yes' resolution even harder to achieve. The market probability typically floats at baseline noise levels for these extreme tail risks (e.g., 1-2%). Given the absence of viable paths for 25th Amendment removal, the probability is anchored at the minimum permitted threshold of 0.02.
